# Twico UI — LLM usage guide (llms.txt)

Twico UI is a free, MIT-licensed **React component library**: 62 components and 23 standalone
hooks (plus the `useToast` hook), with dark mode, motion, accessibility, RTL, a density scale, and
design tokens. **Zero runtime dependencies** (peer: `react`/`react-dom` >= 18).

This file is the authoritative guide for writing application code with the **`twico-ui` npm package**.
The component and hook reference below is generated from the library's own TypeScript types and
component data, so every component, prop, and example reflects the shipped API. When you generate
code that uses Twico UI, follow the setup and conventions here, then use the per-component sections
for exact props.

> Not to be confused with `SKILL.md` / `DESIGN-SYSTEM.md`, which are for generating static HTML
> design mockups from the design-system bundle. This guide is for **real React app code**.

## Install & setup (do this once)

```bash
npm install twico-ui
```

Import the stylesheet **once** at your app root — this is the only CSS you import:

```jsx
import "twico-ui/styles.css";
```

Then import components and hooks by name from the package root:

```jsx
import { Button, Input, Dialog, useColorScheme } from "twico-ui";
```

Rules that matter:

- **Do not import any per-component CSS — there is none.** The single `twico-ui/styles.css` carries
  the design tokens, base reset, and self-hosted fonts; each component injects its own scoped CSS at
  runtime. Importing `twico-ui/styles.css` more than once is harmless but unnecessary.
- The package ships a `"use client"` directive, so it works in the **Next.js App Router / React
  Server Components** out of the box — import it into client trees directly.
- **SSR-safe**: components never touch `window`/`document` during render.
- **Peer deps**: `react` and `react-dom` >= 18 (works with React 18 and 19).
- TypeScript types ship with the package; every component's props type is exported (e.g.
  `ButtonProps`).

## Dark mode

Dark mode is a class/attribute on the `<html>` element — add `class="dark"` (or `data-theme="dark"`):

```html
<html class="dark">
```

Or manage it in React with the built-in hook (persists to `localStorage`, syncs across tabs/instances):

```jsx
import { useColorScheme, Button } from "twico-ui";

function ThemeToggle() {
  const { isDark, toggle } = useColorScheme();
  return <Button variant="ghost" onClick={toggle}>{isDark ? "Light" : "Dark"}</Button>;
}
```

Only the semantic color aliases flip in dark mode; the primitive scales stay constant.

## Theming (design tokens)

Everything is styled through CSS custom properties, so you re-skin by overriding tokens at `:root`
(or any scope) — no component props or config needed:

```css
:root {
  --brand-500: #8b5cf6;   /* rebrand: remap the primary scale */
  --radius-md: 8px;        /* tighter corners everywhere */
}
```

Token families: `--color-*` (semantic: `primary`, `success`, `warning`, `danger`, `info`, `bg`,
`surface`, `text`, `border`, …), `--brand-*` / `--indigo-*` (primary scale, 50–950), `--radius-*`,
`--shadow-*`, `--space-*`, `--text-*`, `--font-*`, `--duration-*` / `--ease-*`. The primitive color
scales are also importable as JS objects: `import { indigo, slate } from "twico-ui/colors"`.

- **Density**: set `data-density="compact"` or `"comfortable"` on any ancestor to retune control
  heights for everything inside — no per-component prop.
- **RTL**: set `dir="rtl"` on any ancestor; components use logical properties and mirror automatically.

## Controlled vs uncontrolled (read before using inputs)

Every form component supports both modes:

- **Uncontrolled**: pass `defaultValue`, read changes via `onChange`.
- **Controlled**: pass `value` + `onChange` and own the state.

The `onChange` payload differs by component kind:

- **Composite controls** (Select, MultiSelect, Combobox, Slider, Rating, Tabs, DatePicker,
  DateRangePicker, ColorPicker, …) call **`onChange(value)`** — you receive the value directly.
- **Native input controls** (Input, Textarea, and the toggle family Checkbox / Switch / Radio) call
  **`onChange(event)`** — read `event.target.value` (or `event.target.checked` for the toggles).
- Currency / CurrencyField are specialized money inputs — see their sections for their exact
  `value`/`onChange` shape.

```jsx
// controlled text input (native — event payload)
const [email, setEmail] = useState("");
<Input value={email} onChange={(e) => setEmail(e.target.value)} placeholder="you@example.com" />

// controlled select (composite — value payload)
const [role, setRole] = useState("editor");
<Select
  value={role}
  onChange={setRole}
  options={[{ value: "admin", label: "Admin" }, { value: "editor", label: "Editor" }]}
/>
```

The `Field` component is a shared label/hint/error/required wrapper for any control. The
`useControllableState` hook implements this exact controlled/uncontrolled pattern if you build your own.

## Overlays (Dialog, Drawer, Menu, Popover, CommandPalette, Tooltip)

- **Modal overlays — Dialog and Drawer — are driven by `open` + `onClose`:**

```jsx
const [open, setOpen] = useState(false);
<Button onClick={() => setOpen(true)}>Open</Button>
<Dialog open={open} onClose={() => setOpen(false)} title="Confirm" description="Are you sure?">
  …body…
</Dialog>
```

- **Anchored overlays — Menu and Popover — take a `trigger` node** and use `open` / `defaultOpen` +
  `onOpenChange` (uncontrolled by default — just pass `trigger` and `items`).
- All portal to `document.body`, animate in **and** out, and dismiss on backdrop click / Escape;
  `prefers-reduced-motion` is respected.
- The `useDisclosure` hook is a convenient open/close state helper for driving these.

## Toasts

Wrap your app **once** in `ToastProvider`, then call the imperative `useToast` hook anywhere beneath
it. `useToast()` returns an object — **destructure `toast`**:

```jsx
import { ToastProvider, useToast, Button } from "twico-ui";

function App() {
  return (
    <ToastProvider limit={4}>
      <Page />
    </ToastProvider>
  );
}

function Page() {
  const { toast } = useToast();
  // toast.success / .error / .warning / .info / .danger(title, options?)
  return <Button onClick={() => toast.success("Saved")}>Save</Button>;
}
```

## Icons

Components take icons as **React nodes** via props like `leftIcon`, `rightIcon`, `icon`, `header` —
bring any icon (inline SVG, or any icon library). Icons inherit `currentColor` and the control's size.

For convenience, **`twico-ui/icons` re-exports the full [Lucide](https://lucide.dev) icon set** (the
visual language Twico UI is built on) **plus 31 curated brand icons** Lucide omits. Lucide icons need
the **optional peer** `lucide-react` (`npm install lucide-react`) and are tree-shakeable; the brand
icons (`GithubIcon`, `VercelIcon`, `FigmaIcon`, `XTwitterIcon`, …) are zero-dependency inline SVG
built in — **no peer needed**. The core `twico-ui` package itself stays zero-dependency. All icons
are plain SVG components (no hooks), so they work in Server Components too. Names use the `*Icon`
suffix; icons take `size` (default 24) and `color` (default `currentColor`).

```jsx
import { HomeIcon, SearchIcon } from "twico-ui/icons"; // Lucide — needs the optional `lucide-react` peer
import { GithubIcon } from "twico-ui/icons";           // brand — no peer needed
<Button leftIcon={<HomeIcon size={16} />}>Home</Button>
<Button variant="outline" leftIcon={<GithubIcon size={16} />}>Star</Button>

// …or pass your own inline SVG (no peer needed):
<Button leftIcon={<svg width="16" height="16" viewBox="0 0 24 24" /* … */ />}>Add item</Button>
```

## Shared prop vocabulary

- `size`: `"sm" | "md" | "lg"` (default `"md"`); a few components add `"xs"`/`"full"`.
- `tone`: semantic color. Status components expose `"primary" | "neutral" | "success" | "warning" |
  "danger" | "info"`; action components (Button, IconButton) expose `"primary" | "danger"`.
- `variant`: fill style — commonly `"solid" | "soft" | "outline" | "ghost"` (the exact set varies per
  component; see each section).
- `value` / `defaultValue` / `onChange`: the controlled/uncontrolled triad.
- `items` / `options`: array-driven content.
- `label` / `hint` / `error` / `required`: form labelling (or use the `Field` wrapper).

**Every component also accepts the standard DOM attributes for its root element** (`className`, `id`,
`style`, `data-*`, `aria-*`) and forwards remaining props and event handlers via `...rest`. To keep
this guide focused, the per-component "Props" lists below show only the **component-specific** props
(the generic DOM passthrough is omitted).

## How to read the reference below

For each component: a one-line summary, its import, the component-specific props as
`` `name` `` — type · *required* or default — what it does, and a copy-paste example. Object-shaped
types referenced in props (e.g. `SelectOption`, `AccordionItem`, `DatatableColumn`) are illustrated
in the examples.

---

## Component reference (62 components)

### Buttons & actions

#### Button

Button is a primary action button with solid, soft, outline, and ghost variants crossed with a primary or danger tone, four sizes (xs/sm/md/lg), loading state, optional left/right icons, and a click ripple effect. Use it for primary and secondary actions throughout the UI.

`import { Button } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `variant` — `"solid" | "soft" | "outline" | "ghost"` · default `"solid"` — Fill style: solid (primary CTA), soft (tinted), outline, or ghost. Cross with `tone` for color.
- `tone` — `"primary" | "danger"` · default `"primary"` — Color/intent, orthogonal to variant — primary by default; tone="danger" makes a destructive button in any variant.
- `size` — `"xs" | "sm" | "md" | "lg"` · default `"md"` — Adds a new "xs" size (~26px tall, smaller than "sm") to the existing union; sm/md/lg are unchanged. xs height is composed locally from space tokens (no global control-h-xs token).
- `leftIcon` — `React.ReactNode` — Renders an icon node before the label, commonly used to reinforce the action with a leading visual cue.
- `rightIcon` — `React.ReactNode` — Renders an icon node after the label, often a chevron or arrow signaling navigation or progression.
- `loading` — `boolean` · default `false` — When true, swaps in a spinner and disables interaction to signal an in-flight action and prevent duplicate clicks.
- `fullWidth` — `boolean` · default `false` — Stretches the button to fill its container's width, handy for full-width CTAs and stacked mobile action buttons.
- `as` — `"button" | "a"` · default `"button"` — Chooses whether to render a native "button" or an "a" anchor, letting the button act as a real link when needed.
- `children` — `React.ReactNode` — The label content rendered inside the button, typically text but accepting any inline node alongside the icons.
- `href` — `string` — Link destination — only used with as="a"; scheme-sanitized (javascript:/data:/vbscript: render without href).
- `target` — `React.HTMLAttributeAnchorTarget` — Anchor target — only used with as="a" (e.g. "_blank").
- `rel` — `string` — Anchor rel — only used with as="a"; pair "noopener noreferrer" with target="_blank".
- `onClick` — `(e: React.MouseEvent) => void` — Click handler — fires on click/tap and on Enter or Space.
- `type` — `"button" | "submit" | "reset"` · default `"button"` — Native button type — use "submit" to submit a surrounding form.
- `disabled` — `boolean` · default `false` — Disables the control — no interaction and a dimmed appearance.
- `autoFocus` — `boolean` · default `false` — Focus the element automatically when it mounts.

```jsx
import { Button } from "twico-ui";

const PlusIcon = () => (<svg width="16" height="16" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" strokeWidth="2" strokeLinecap="round"><path d="M12 5v14M5 12h14" /></svg>);

<Button variant="solid" size="md" onClick={() => {}}>Save changes</Button>
<Button variant="soft" leftIcon={<PlusIcon />}>Add item</Button>
<Button variant="outline">Cancel</Button>
<Button variant="ghost">Skip</Button>
<Button tone="danger" loading>Deleting…</Button>
```

#### IconButton

IconButton is a square or circular button that renders a single icon and always requires an aria-label for accessibility. Use it in toolbars, cards, and dense UI where an icon-only control is clearer than a labeled button.

`import { IconButton } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `icon` — `React.ReactNode` — Supplies the single icon node to render, such as a Lucide SVG, falling back to children when omitted.
- `variant` — `"solid" | "soft" | "outline" | "ghost"` · default `"ghost"` — Fill style (same axes as Button): solid, soft, outline, or ghost; ghost by default. Cross with `tone` for color.
- `tone` — `"primary" | "danger"` · default `"primary"` — Color/intent, orthogonal to variant (mirrors Button) — primary by default; tone="danger" makes an icon-only destructive button in any variant.
- `size` — `"xs" | "sm" | "md" | "lg"` · default `"md"` — Adds a new "xs" size (~26px square, smaller than "sm") to the existing union; sm/md/lg are unchanged.
- `round` — `boolean` · default `false` — Renders a fully rounded circular button when true instead of the default squared shape with rounded corners.
- `aria-label` — `string` · **required** — Provides the required accessible name for this icon-only button so screen readers announce its purpose.
- `onClick` — `(e: React.MouseEvent) => void` — Click handler — fires on click/tap and on Enter or Space.
- `type` — `"button" | "submit" | "reset"` · default `"button"` — Native button type — use "submit" to submit a surrounding form.
- `disabled` — `boolean` · default `false` — Disables the control — no interaction and a dimmed appearance.
- `autoFocus` — `boolean` · default `false` — Focus the element automatically when it mounts.

```jsx
import { IconButton } from "twico-ui";

const SettingsIcon = () => (<svg width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" strokeWidth="2" strokeLinecap="round"><path d="M4 21v-7M4 10V3M12 21v-9M12 8V3M20 21v-5M20 12V3M1 14h6M9 8h6M17 16h6" /></svg>);
const HeartIcon = () => (<svg width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" strokeWidth="2"><path d="M20.8 4.6a5.5 5.5 0 0 0-7.8 0L12 5.7l-1.1-1.1a5.5 5.5 0 1 0-7.8 7.8L12 21l8.9-8.6a5.5 5.5 0 0 0 0-7.8z" /></svg>);
const TrashIcon = () => (<svg width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" strokeWidth="2" strokeLinecap="round"><path d="M3 6h18M8 6V4h8v2M19 6l-1 14H6L5 6" /></svg>);

<IconButton aria-label="Settings" icon={<SettingsIcon />} />
<IconButton aria-label="Like" variant="soft" round icon={<HeartIcon />} />
<IconButton aria-label="Delete" variant="outline" icon={<TrashIcon />} />
```

### Layout

#### AppShell

Full-height application shell: a fixed sidebar beside a scrollable content area with an optional fixed topbar. The content fills the remaining width and scrolls independently, so the sidebar and header stay put.

`import { AppShell } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `sidebar` — `React.ReactNode` — The sidebar element (e.g. a Sidebar), rendered full-height on the inline-start side of the shell.
- `header` — `React.ReactNode` — Topbar content in a fixed header above the content; a flex row with space-between, so pass a left and a right group.
- `children` — `React.ReactNode` — Main content; fills the remaining space and scrolls independently of the fixed sidebar and header.
- `height` — `string | number` · default `"100dvh"` — Overall shell height; pass a fixed value to embed the shell inside a smaller region (e.g. a demo).
- `padded` — `boolean` · default `true` — Pads the scrollable content region with the standard page padding; set false for edge-to-edge content.

```jsx
import { AppShell, Sidebar, Button } from "twico-ui";

<AppShell
  sidebar={
    <Sidebar
      brand={<b>Acme</b>}
      items={[{ label: "Home", active: true }, { label: "Settings" }]}
    />
  }
  header={
    <>
      <b>Dashboard</b>
      <Button size="sm">New</Button>
    </>
  }
>
  <h1>Welcome back</h1>
</AppShell>
```

#### Box

Generic, token-styled box — the building block for non-flex layout (padding, margin, background, border, radius, shadow) without writing CSS.

`import { Box } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `p, px, py, pt, pr, pb, pl` — `number | string` — Sets inner padding on the matching side(s), accepting a spacing-scale step (number) or any raw CSS length string.
- `m, mx, my, mt, mr, mb, ml` — `number | string` — Sets outer margin on the matching side(s), accepting a spacing-scale step (number) or any raw CSS length string.
- `bg` — `"surface" | "surface-raised" | "surface-sunken" | "bg" | string` — Sets the background to a surface token like "surface" or "surface-raised", or to any custom CSS color value.
- `border` — `boolean` · default `false` — When true, draws a 1px border using the theme's border token, useful for visually separating the box from its surroundings.
- `radius` — `string` — Applies a border-radius from the radius token scale by suffix (e.g. "lg") to round the box's corners.
- `shadow` — `string` — Applies an elevation box-shadow from the shadow token scale by suffix (e.g. "md") to lift the box off the page.
- `as` — `ElementType` · default `"div"` — Sets which element or tag the box renders as, defaulting to "div" but accepting any semantic HTML element type.
- `p` — `number | string` — Padding on all sides — a spacing-scale step (number) or any CSS length.
- `px` — `number | string` — Horizontal padding (inline start + end) — spacing step or CSS length.
- `py` — `number | string` — Vertical padding (block start + end) — spacing step or CSS length.
- `pt` — `number | string` — Padding top — spacing step or CSS length.
- `pr` — `number | string` — Padding right (inline end) — spacing step or CSS length.
- `pb` — `number | string` — Padding bottom — spacing step or CSS length.
- `pl` — `number | string` — Padding left (inline start) — spacing step or CSS length.
- `m` — `number | string` — Margin on all sides — a spacing-scale step (number) or any CSS length.
- `mx` — `number | string` — Horizontal margin (inline start + end) — spacing step or CSS length.
- `my` — `number | string` — Vertical margin (block start + end) — spacing step or CSS length.
- `mt` — `number | string` — Margin top — spacing step or CSS length.
- `mr` — `number | string` — Margin right (inline end) — spacing step or CSS length.
- `mb` — `number | string` — Margin bottom — spacing step or CSS length.
- `ml` — `number | string` — Margin left (inline start) — spacing step or CSS length.

```jsx
import { Box } from "twico-ui";

<Box p={4} bg="surface" border radius="lg" shadow="sm">
  Padded, bordered surface.
</Box>
```

#### Container

Centers content and caps its width with responsive horizontal padding — the outer wrapper for a page or section.

`import { Container } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `size` — `"sm" | "md" | "lg" | "xl" | "full" | string` · default `"lg"` — Caps the maximum width using a named token (sm through xl, or full) or any CSS length, defaulting to "lg".
- `padded` — `boolean` · default `true` — Adds responsive horizontal padding so content does not touch the viewport edges; enabled by default, set false to remove it.
- `as` — `ElementType` · default `"div"` — Chooses the rendered HTML element or component (such as "section" or "main"), defaulting to "div" for semantic flexibility.

```jsx
import { Container, Heading } from "twico-ui";

<Container size="lg">
  <Heading level={1}>Page title</Heading>
</Container>
```

#### Divider

Divider is a thin separator rule that visually splits content, either horizontally or vertically. Use it to break up sections, lists, or inline items, optionally with a centered or aligned label (e.g. "OR").

`import { Divider } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `orientation` — `"horizontal" | "vertical"` · default `"horizontal"` — Direction of the separator rule, either horizontal (default) to split stacked content or vertical for inline items.
- `inset` — `boolean` · default `false` — Indents the start of a horizontal rule to align it with inset content like list item text.
- `align` — `"left" | "center" | "right" | "start" | "end"` · default `"center"` — Label alignment when children are provided; logical start/end now accepted and map to left/right (additive — existing left/center/right unchanged).
- `children` — `React.ReactNode` — Optional label text rendered within the rule, such as "OR", to break content with a caption.

```jsx
import { Divider } from "twico-ui";

<Divider />

<Divider>OR</Divider>

<span>A</span>
<Divider orientation="vertical" />
<span>B</span>
```

#### Grid

CSS grid primitive — a responsive auto-fill grid (minChildWidth) or a fixed column count (columns).

`import { Grid } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `minChildWidth` — `number | string` — Enables a responsive auto-fill grid where columns wrap once children fall below this minimum width (number is treated as px).
- `columns` — `number` — Sets a fixed number of equal columns, used when minChildWidth is unset for predictable non-responsive layouts.
- `gap` — `number | string` · default `4` — Controls spacing between grid cells as a spacing-step number or any CSS length, defaulting to step 4.
- `align` — `string` — Maps to the CSS align-items value, controlling how children are aligned along the block (vertical) axis.
- `justify` — `string` — Maps to the CSS justify-items value, controlling how children are aligned along the inline (horizontal) axis.
- `as` — `ElementType` · default `"div"` — Renders the grid as a different element or tag instead of the default div, for semantic markup.

```jsx
import { Grid, Card } from "twico-ui";

<Grid minChildWidth={220} gap={4}>
  <Card>One</Card>
  <Card>Two</Card>
  <Card>Three</Card>
</Grid>
```

#### Stack

Flexbox layout primitive — arranges children in a row or column with token-based gaps. Reach for it instead of hand-written flex divs.

`import { Stack } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `direction` — `"row" | "column"` · default `"column"` — Sets the main-axis flow, stacking children vertically with "column" (default) or side by side with "row".
- `gap` — `number | string` · default `4` — Sets spacing between children, as a numeric spacing step mapped to --space-* or any raw CSS length string.
- `align` — `string` — Maps to the align-items value, controlling how children line up along the cross axis.
- `justify` — `string` — Maps to the justify-content value, controlling how children are distributed along the main axis.
- `wrap` — `boolean` · default `false` — Allows children to wrap onto multiple lines instead of overflowing when they exceed the container width.
- `inline` — `boolean` · default `false` — Renders the stack as inline-flex rather than block-level flex, so it sits inline with surrounding content.
- `as` — `ElementType` · default `"div"` — Overrides the rendered element or tag (default "div"), useful for semantic wrappers like nav, ul, or section.

```jsx
import { Stack } from "twico-ui";

<Stack direction="row" gap={3} align="center" wrap>
  <Button>Save</Button>
  <Button variant="ghost">Cancel</Button>
</Stack>
```

### Typography

#### Code

Inline code with a mono font and a subtle token-styled surface — use it instead of a bare code tag.

`import { Code } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `as` — `ElementType` · default `"code"` — Overrides the rendered element, defaulting to a code tag but allowing any tag when the semantics differ.
- `children` — `ReactNode` — Provides the inline code text or nodes shown in the mono font on the subtle token-styled surface.

```jsx
import { Code, Text } from "twico-ui";

<Text>Install with <Code>npm install twico-ui</Code>.</Text>
```

#### Heading

Heading (h1–h6) with consistent token typography. Use it instead of bare heading tags.

`import { Heading } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `level` — `1 | 2 | 3 | 4 | 5 | 6` · default `2` — Chooses which h1-h6 tag renders and its default token size, keeping document hierarchy and typography consistent.
- `size` — `string` — Overrides the font-size token suffix (e.g. "3xl") to decouple visual scale from the semantic heading level.
- `align` — `string` — Sets the CSS text-align value, letting you left-align, center, or right-align the heading text.
- `as` — `ElementType` — Renders a different tag while preserving the level's size, useful when semantics and visual weight should differ.

```jsx
import { Heading } from "twico-ui";

<Heading level={1}>Dashboard</Heading>
<Heading level={3}>Recent activity</Heading>
```

#### Text

Body text with token sizes and semantic color tones. Use it instead of bare paragraph/span tags.

`import { Text } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `size` — `"xs" | "sm" | "base" | "lg" | "xl"` · default `"base"` — Picks the font-size token from xs to xl, scaling the body text to fit its context, base by default.
- `tone` — `"default" | "muted" | "subtle" | "primary" | "success" | "warning" | "danger" | "info" | "neutral"` · default `"default"` — Sets the text color — neutral roles (default, muted, subtle) plus the six semantic color intents.
- `weight` — `string` — Sets the font-weight token suffix like semibold to adjust how heavy the text appears.
- `align` — `string` — Sets the text-align value, controlling whether the text reads left, center, right, or justified.
- `as` — `ElementType` · default `"p"` — Chooses the element or tag rendered, defaulting to p so you can swap in span or other tags.

```jsx
import { Text } from "twico-ui";

<Text>Default paragraph text.</Text>
<Text size="sm" tone="muted">A muted caption.</Text>
```

### Inputs

#### Checkbox

An accessible checkbox with an animated checkmark, optional label and description, and an indeterminate (mixed) state. Use it for binary on/off choices in forms, or as a "select all" control that can show a partially-selected dash.

`import { Checkbox } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `label` — `React.ReactNode` — Renders text or a node beside the box, serving as the clickable caption that describes what the checkbox toggles.
- `description` — `React.ReactNode` — Shows secondary helper text below the label to clarify the option's meaning or consequences without crowding it.
- `indeterminate` — `boolean` · default `false` — Displays the mixed dash state instead of a check, ideal for a select-all that is only partially selected.
- `size` — `"sm" | "md"` · default `"md"` — Adjusts the checkbox dimensions between sm and md to match the density of the surrounding form layout.
- `tone` — `"primary" | "success" | "warning" | "danger" | "info" | "neutral"` · default `"primary"` — Sets the accent color of the checked/active state from six semantic intents like success, danger, or info.
- `checked` — `boolean` — Drives the checked state in controlled mode, requiring an onChange handler to keep the input in sync.
- `defaultChecked` — `boolean` — Sets the initial checked state for uncontrolled usage, after which the input manages its own value internally.
- `disabled` — `boolean` · default `false` — Blocks interaction and dims the control when true, signaling the option is currently unavailable to the user.
- `onChange` — `React.ChangeEventHandler<HTMLInputElement>` — Fires on every toggle with the native change event, letting you read the new checked state and update state.
- `id` — `string` — Sets the input's id used to associate the label, auto-generated when omitted so the click target stays connected.
- `className` — `string` · default `""` — Appends extra class names to the wrapping label element for custom spacing, alignment, or layout overrides.
- `error` — `React.ReactNode` — Error message shown below the control; tints the box red and wires aria-invalid/aria-describedby.
- `name` — `string` — Form field name submitted with the value.
- `placeholder` — `string` — Placeholder text shown while the field is empty.
- `readOnly` — `boolean` · default `false` — Makes the field non-editable while still focusable/selectable.
- `required` — `boolean` · default `false` — Marks the field required for native form validation.
- `autoComplete` — `string` — Browser autocomplete hint (e.g. "email", "off").

```jsx
import { Checkbox } from "twico-ui";

<Checkbox label="Subscribe to updates" defaultChecked />
<Checkbox label="Select all" indeterminate />
<Checkbox
  label="Notifications"
  description="Get email alerts for new activity"
/>
<Checkbox label="Disabled option" disabled />
<Checkbox label="Compact" size="sm" />
```

#### ColorPicker

ColorPicker is a popover-based color input featuring a saturation/value square, a hue slider, a hex text field, and preset swatches, with the value represented as a hex string. Use it when you need users to choose a color (e.g. a brand or theme color), either controlled via value/onChange or uncontrolled via defaultValue.

`import { ColorPicker } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `label` — `React.ReactNode` — Renders an optional caption above the trigger to describe which color the picker controls, such as a brand color.
- `tone` — `"primary" | "success" | "warning" | "danger" | "info" | "neutral"` · default `"primary"` — Recolors the hex-input focus accent using one of six semantic intents; defaults to primary.
- `value` — `string` — Sets the selected hex color in controlled mode, pairing with onChange to keep the displayed color in sync.
- `defaultValue` — `string` · default `"#6366F1"` — Provides the initial hex color for uncontrolled usage, after which the picker tracks selection internally.
- `presets` — `string[]` · default `8 default swatches (e.g. #6366F1, #0EA5E9, #14B8A6, ...)` — Lists hex swatch colors shown below the picker for quick one-click selection of common or brand-approved colors.
- `onChange` — `(hex: string) => void` — Fires with the new hex string whenever the user adjusts saturation, hue, the text field, or picks a preset.
- `disabled` — `boolean` · default `false` — Disable all interaction (the popover cannot be opened).
- `hint` — `React.ReactNode` — Helper text shown below when there is no error.
- `error` — `React.ReactNode` — Error message — turns the field red and replaces the hint.
- `required` — `boolean` · default `false` — Marks the field required (adds an asterisk to the label).

```jsx
import { ColorPicker } from "twico-ui";

// Controlled
const [color, setColor] = React.useState("#6366F1");
<ColorPicker label="Brand color" value={color} onChange={setColor} />

// Uncontrolled with custom presets
<ColorPicker
  defaultValue="#14B8A6"
  presets={["#6366F1", "#14B8A6", "#F43F5E"]}
/>
```

#### Combobox

Combobox is a searchable single-select dropdown (MUI-Autocomplete style) where you type directly in the field to filter options, with support for grouped options and two-line (title + subtitle) entries. Use it when a select list is long enough that users benefit from typeahead filtering rather than scrolling.

`import { Combobox } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `label` — `React.ReactNode` — Renders a caption above the control to identify the field and improve accessibility for the searchable select.
- `hint` — `React.ReactNode` — Shows helper text below the field to guide input, displayed only while no error message is present.
- `error` — `React.ReactNode` — Displays an error message, marks the field invalid, and replaces the hint to flag a failed validation.
- `required` — `boolean` · default `false` — Adds a required asterisk next to the label, signaling that the user must select a value before submitting.
- `size` — `"sm" | "md" | "lg"` · default `"md"` — Sets the control height across sm, md, or lg to match the density of the surrounding form.
- `tone` — `"primary" | "success" | "warning" | "danger" | "info" | "neutral"` · default `"primary"` — Recolors the focus/open accent (border + ring) using one of six semantic intents; defaults to primary.
- `placeholder` — `string` · default `"Select…"` — Sets the muted text shown in the empty field before the user types to filter or selects an option.
- `options` — `Array<string | ComboboxOption | ComboboxGroup>` · **required** — Defines the selectable items as plain strings, value/label/description objects, or grouped option collections.
- `value` — `string | null` — Sets the selected option's value in controlled mode, pairing with onChange to keep the field in sync.
- `defaultValue` — `string | null` · default `null` — Provides the initial selection for uncontrolled usage, defaulting to null so the field starts empty.
- `onChange` — `(value: string | null) => void` — Fires with the newly selected value, or null when the selection is cleared, so you can update state.
- `clearable` — `boolean` · default `false` — Adds a clear button when a value is selected, letting users reset the field back to an empty selection.
- `disabled` — `boolean` · default `false` — Blocks typing and selection while dimming the field, indicating the control is currently unavailable.
- `placement` — `"bottom" | "top"` · default `"bottom"` — Open the menu upward instead of down (e.g. near a viewport bottom).
- `portal` — `boolean` · default `false` — Render the dropdown in a portal (position:fixed on document.body) so it is never clipped by a scrolling/overflow-hidden ancestor; auto-flips up near the viewport bottom.
- `minWidth` — `number` · default `0` — Minimum popover width in px when portaled (useful when the control is narrow).
- `name` — `string` — Form field name submitted with the value.
- `readOnly` — `boolean` · default `false` — Makes the field non-editable while still focusable/selectable.
- `autoComplete` — `string` — Browser autocomplete hint (e.g. "email", "off").

```jsx
import { Combobox } from "twico-ui";

const [country, setCountry] = useState(null);

<Combobox
  label="Country"
  placeholder="Search a country"
  clearable
  value={country}
  onChange={setCountry}
  options={[
    { group: "Asia", options: [
      { value: "id", label: "Indonesia", description: "Jakarta" },
      { value: "jp", label: "Japan", description: "Tokyo" },
    ]},
    { group: "Europe", options: [
      { value: "de", label: "Germany", description: "Berlin" },
    ]},
  ]}
/>
```

#### Currency

Currency is a text input for monetary amounts with a fixed currency defined in code: it renders the currency symbol as a prefix, the currency code as a suffix, and enforces the currency's decimal precision as you type. Use it when the currency is known up front (use CurrencyField when the user should pick one).

`import { Currency } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `label` — `React.ReactNode` — Renders a label above the input describing the monetary field, accepting any ReactNode for icons or rich text.
- `hint` — `React.ReactNode` — Shows helper text below the input to guide entry, displayed only when no error is present.
- `error` — `React.ReactNode` — Displays an error message below the input and marks the field invalid, overriding any hint while set.
- `required` — `boolean` · default `false` — Marks the field as required and renders an asterisk beside the label; off by default.
- `size` — `"sm" | "md" | "lg"` · default `"md"` — Sets the input height and padding (sm, md, or lg) to match the surrounding form's density, defaulting to "md".
- `tone` — `"primary" | "success" | "warning" | "danger" | "info" | "neutral"` · default `"primary"` — Recolors the focus/open accent (border + ring) using one of six semantic intents; defaults to primary.
- `currency` — `string` · default `"USD"` — Selects the fixed currency by code from the built-in table (e.g. USD, EUR, IDR, JPY), driving symbol and precision.
- `precision` — `number` — Overrides the number of decimal digits enforced while typing, instead of the currency's default precision.
- `symbol` — `string` — Overrides the prefix symbol shown before the amount, useful when the built-in symbol for the currency isn't desired.
- `code` — `string` — Overrides the suffix code displayed after the amount, replacing the default currency code label.
- `value` — `string | number` — Sets the controlled amount as a string or number, keeping the input value driven entirely by your state.
- `defaultValue` — `string | number` · default `""` — Sets the initial amount for uncontrolled usage, after which the input manages its own value; defaults to an empty string.
- `onChange` — `(e: React.ChangeEvent<HTMLInputElement>) => void` — The native change handler receiving the raw input event, useful when you need the underlying DOM event.
- `onValueChange` — `(value: number | null, formatted: string) => void` — Fires with the parsed numeric value (or null) and the formatted display string whenever the amount changes.
- `name` — `string` — Form field name submitted with the value.
- `placeholder` — `string` — Placeholder text shown while the field is empty.
- `disabled` — `boolean` · default `false` — Disables the control — no interaction and a dimmed appearance.
- `readOnly` — `boolean` · default `false` — Makes the field non-editable while still focusable/selectable.
- `autoComplete` — `string` — Browser autocomplete hint (e.g. "email", "off").

```jsx
import { Currency } from "twico-ui";

const [price, setPrice] = useState(0);

<Currency label="Price" currency="USD" value={price} onValueChange={setPrice} />
<Currency label="Harga" currency="IDR" />
<Currency label="Total" currency="JPY" />
<Currency label="Custom" symbol="₿" code="BTC" precision={8} />
```

#### CurrencyField

CurrencyField is a currency amount input where the user picks the currency from a searchable dropdown, and that choice drives the prefix symbol, suffix code, and enforced decimal precision. Use it when you need to capture a monetary amount whose currency can be chosen and changed by the user (switching currency re-clamps the amount to the new precision).

`import { CurrencyField } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `label` — `React.ReactNode` — Renders a label above the field describing the amount, accepting any ReactNode for icons or formatted text.
- `hint` — `React.ReactNode` — Shows helper text below the field to guide entry, displayed only when no error message is present.
- `error` — `React.ReactNode` — Displays an error message and marks the field invalid when set, taking visual precedence over the hint.
- `required` — `boolean` · default `false` — Marks the field as required and renders an asterisk beside the label; off by default.
- `size` — `"sm" | "md" | "lg"` · default `"md"` — Sets the input and dropdown height (sm, md, or lg) to match the surrounding form's density, defaulting to "md".
- `tone` — `"primary" | "success" | "warning" | "danger" | "info" | "neutral"` · default `"primary"` — Recolors the focus/open accent (border + ring) using one of six semantic intents; defaults to primary.
- `currency` — `string` — Controls the selected currency code from your state, keeping the chosen currency driven externally.
- `defaultCurrency` — `string` · default `"USD"` — Sets the initially selected currency code for uncontrolled usage, defaulting to "USD".
- `onCurrencyChange` — `(code: string) => void` — Fires with the new code when the user picks a different currency from the searchable dropdown.
- `currencies` — `Array<string | CurrencyOption>` — Restricts the selectable currencies to the given codes or {value,label} options, instead of the full built-in list.
- `value` — `string | number` — Sets the controlled amount as a string or number, keeping the entered value driven entirely by your state.
- `defaultValue` — `string | number` · default `""` — Sets the initial amount for uncontrolled usage, after which the field manages its own value; defaults to an empty string.
- `onValueChange` — `(value: number | null, formatted: string, currency: string) => void` — Fires with the parsed number (or null), the formatted string, and the active currency code whenever either changes.
- `onChange` — `(e: React.ChangeEvent<HTMLInputElement>) => void` — Native change handler (receives the event); fires alongside onValueChange, matching Currency. Previously typed-out via Omit and silently dropped at runtime.
- `name` — `string` — Form field name submitted with the value.
- `placeholder` — `string` — Placeholder text shown while the field is empty.
- `disabled` — `boolean` · default `false` — Disables the control — no interaction and a dimmed appearance.
- `readOnly` — `boolean` · default `false` — Makes the field non-editable while still focusable/selectable.
- `autoComplete` — `string` — Browser autocomplete hint (e.g. "email", "off").

```jsx
import { CurrencyField } from "twico-ui";

<CurrencyField
  label="Amount"
  defaultCurrency="USD"
  onValueChange={(n, str, code) => {}}
/>

{/* limit the currency choices */}
<CurrencyField
  label="Amount"
  defaultCurrency="EUR"
  currencies={["USD", "EUR", "GBP", "IDR"]}
/>
```

#### DatePicker

DatePicker is a calendar-popover date input with month/year navigation, a month grid, today highlight, min/max bounds, and a clearable value. Use it when you need users to select a single date with optional range limits and custom formatting.

`import { DatePicker } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `label` — `React.ReactNode` — Optional field label rendered above the input to name the control and associate it for accessibility.
- `tone` — `"primary" | "success" | "warning" | "danger" | "info" | "neutral"` · default `"primary"` — Recolors the focus/open accent (border + ring) using one of six semantic intents; defaults to primary.
- `value` — `Date | null` — Controlled selected date, or null when empty; pair it with onChange to own the value in state.
- `defaultValue` — `Date | null` · default `null` — Initial date for uncontrolled usage when value is omitted, defaulting to null so the field starts empty.
- `placeholder` — `string` · default `"Select date"` — Text shown in the trigger when no date is selected, defaulting to "Select date" to prompt the user.
- `min` — `Date` — Earliest selectable date; dates before it are disabled in the calendar grid to enforce a lower bound.
- `max` — `Date` — Latest selectable date; dates after it are disabled in the calendar grid to enforce an upper bound.
- `disabled` — `boolean` · default `false` — Disables the control and prevents the calendar popover from opening, useful for read-only or gated form states.
- `clearable` — `boolean` · default `true` — Shows a clear (×) button when a date is set so users can reset the field, enabled by default.
- `format` — `(date: Date) => string` — Custom formatter that turns the selected Date into display text, defaulting to a localized medium date string.
- `weekStartsOn` — `0 | 1 | 2 | 3 | 4 | 5 | 6` · default `0` — Widened from 0|1 to any weekday; controls which day the calendar grid + weekday header start on (0 = Sunday).
- `onChange` — `(date: Date | null) => void` — Fires with the newly selected Date, or null when the value is cleared, so you can update state.
- `hint` — `React.ReactNode` — Helper text shown below when there is no error.
- `error` — `React.ReactNode` — Error message — turns the field red and replaces the hint.
- `required` — `boolean` · default `false` — Marks the field required (adds an asterisk to the label).
- `locale` — `string` — BCP-47 locale for Intl-derived month/weekday names and date formatting; omit for the runtime default.

```jsx
import { DatePicker } from "twico-ui";

const [date, setDate] = useState(null);

<DatePicker
  label="Start date"
  value={date}
  onChange={setDate}
  min={new Date()}
  weekStartsOn={1}
/>
```

#### DateRangePicker

DateRangePicker is a calendar input for selecting a start and end date with click-start/click-end selection, in-range highlighting, hover preview, and quick presets (Last 7/14/30/90 days). Use it when a form needs a reporting period or any bounded date span.

`import { DateRangePicker } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `label` — `React.ReactNode` — Optional field label rendered above the control to name the date range input and aid accessibility.
- `tone` — `"primary" | "success" | "warning" | "danger" | "info" | "neutral"` · default `"primary"` — Recolors the focus/open accent (border + ring) using one of six semantic intents; defaults to primary.
- `value` — `DateRange` — Controlled range as a { start, end } object; pair it with onChange to own the selection in state.
- `defaultValue` — `DateRange` · default `{ start: null, end: null }` — Initial range for uncontrolled usage, defaulting to { start: null, end: null } so the field starts empty.
- `placeholder` — `string` · default `"Select date range"` — Text shown in the trigger when no range is selected, defaulting to "Select date range" to prompt the user.
- `presets` — `boolean` · default `true` — Shows the quick-preset column (Last 7/14/30/90 days) for fast range selection, enabled by default.
- `weekStartsOn` — `0 | 1 | 2 | 3 | 4 | 5 | 6` · default `0` — Widened from 0|1 to any weekday; controls which day the calendar grid + weekday header start on (0 = Sunday).
- `onChange` — `(range: DateRange) => void` — Fires with the updated { start, end } range whenever the selection changes so you can update state.
- `disabled` — `boolean` · default `false` — Disable all interaction (the popover cannot be opened).
- `hint` — `React.ReactNode` — Helper text shown below when there is no error.
- `error` — `React.ReactNode` — Error message — turns the field red and replaces the hint.
- `required` — `boolean` · default `false` — Marks the field required (adds an asterisk to the label).
- `locale` — `string` — BCP-47 locale for Intl-derived month/weekday names and date formatting; omit for the runtime default.

```jsx
import { DateRangePicker } from "twico-ui";

const [range, setRange] = useState({ start: null, end: null });

<DateRangePicker
  label="Reporting period"
  value={range}
  onChange={setRange}
  weekStartsOn={1}
/>
```

#### Field

Field is the reusable form-field wrapper that supplies the label, required asterisk, helper hint, and error message (with the matching ARIA ids) that Input and Select already use internally. Wrap any control with it to get consistent field spacing, the danger-colored error treatment, and the aria-describedby target without re-implementing the markup.

`import { Field } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `label` — `React.ReactNode` — Field label rendered above the control.
- `hint` — `React.ReactNode` — Helper text shown below when there is no error.
- `error` — `React.ReactNode` — Error message — replaces the hint and renders in the danger color.
- `required` — `boolean` · default `false` — Marks the field required, adding an asterisk after the label.
- `htmlFor` — `string` — Id of the control the <label> binds to (sets the label's htmlFor).
- `size` — `"sm" | "md" | "lg"` · default `"md"` — Spacing scale for parity with Input/Select; exposed via data-size on the root.
- `id` — `string` · default `auto` — Base id; the hint/error element gets `${id}-desc` for your control's aria-describedby.
- `children` — `React.ReactNode` — The control(s) the field wraps.

```jsx
import { Field } from "twico-ui";

// Wrap any control for the shared label/hint/error chrome. Give Field an id and set
// the control's aria-describedby to that id followed by -desc to announce hint/error.
<Field label="Email" id="email" hint="We'll never share it." required htmlFor="email">
  <input id="email" type="email" aria-describedby="email-desc" />
</Field>

<Field label="Token" id="tok" error="That token is invalid." htmlFor="tok">
  <input id="tok" aria-invalid aria-describedby="tok-desc" />
</Field>
```

#### FileUpload

FileUpload is a drag-and-drop dropzone with a click-to-browse fallback and a removable list of selected files. Use it when you need users to attach one or more files, either controlled via value/onChange or uncontrolled.

`import { FileUpload } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `accept` — `string` — Restricts selectable files using a native input accept string such as "image/*,.pdf", filtering the browse dialog and drops.
- `tone` — `"primary" | "success" | "warning" | "danger" | "info" | "neutral"` · default `"primary"` — Recolors the dropzone focus ring and drag-active border using one of six semantic intents; defaults to primary.
- `multiple` — `boolean` · default `false` — Allows selecting and attaching more than one file at a time when true; defaults to single-file selection.
- `disabled` — `boolean` · default `false` — Disables the dropzone and underlying input when true, blocking clicks, drops, and any further file changes.
- `hint` — `React.ReactNode` — Renders a hint line under the prompt, defaulting to the accepted types or "Any file" when not provided.
- `value` — `File[]` — Supplies the controlled array of selected File objects, making the component fully managed by parent state.
- `onChange` — `(files: File[]) => void` — Fires with the updated File array whenever files are added or removed, letting you sync controlled state.
- `defaultValue` — `File[]` · default `[]` — Uncontrolled initial file list.
- `label` — `React.ReactNode` — Field label rendered above the dropzone.
- `error` — `React.ReactNode` — Error message shown below the dropzone; turns the border red (sets aria-invalid).
- `required` — `boolean` · default `false` — Marks the field required (adds an asterisk to the label).

```jsx
import { FileUpload } from "twico-ui";

const [files, setFiles] = React.useState([]);

<FileUpload
  multiple
  accept="image/*,.pdf"
  value={files}
  onChange={setFiles}
  hint="PNG, JPG or PDF up to 10MB"
/>
```

#### Input

A text input with optional label, hint text, error/validation state, and inline leading/trailing icons. Use it for any single-line form field; with type="password" it gets a built-in reveal/hide eye toggle.

`import { Input } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `label` — `React.ReactNode` — Renders a label node above the control so the field has an accessible, visible name for the user.
- `hint` — `React.ReactNode` — Shows helper text below the input to guide entry; it is hidden once an error message is present.
- `error` — `React.ReactNode` — Displays a validation error below the field, switches the control to its red invalid state, and replaces the hint.
- `required` — `boolean` · default `false` — Marks the field as required and appends an asterisk to the label to signal mandatory input; defaults to false.
- `size` — `"sm" | "md" | "lg"` · default `"md"` — Sets the control height and padding (sm, md, or lg) to match the density of the surrounding form.
- `tone` — `"primary" | "success" | "warning" | "danger" | "info" | "neutral"` · default `"primary"` — Recolors the focus/open accent (border + ring) using one of six semantic intents; defaults to primary.
- `leftIcon` — `React.ReactNode` — Renders an icon node inside the field on the leading edge, useful as a prefix like a search or mail glyph.
- `rightIcon` — `React.ReactNode` — Renders a trailing icon node inside the field, overriding the built-in password reveal toggle when supplied.
- `onChange` — `(e: React.ChangeEvent) => void` — Native change handler on the underlying form control.
- `name` — `string` — Form field name submitted with the value.
- `placeholder` — `string` — Placeholder text shown while the field is empty.
- `disabled` — `boolean` · default `false` — Disables the control — no interaction and a dimmed appearance.
- `readOnly` — `boolean` · default `false` — Makes the field non-editable while still focusable/selectable.
- `autoComplete` — `string` — Browser autocomplete hint (e.g. "email", "off").

```jsx
import { Input } from "twico-ui";

const SearchIcon = () => (<svg width="16" height="16" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" strokeWidth="2" strokeLinecap="round"><circle cx="11" cy="11" r="8" /><path d="m21 21-4.3-4.3" /></svg>);

<Input label="Email" type="email" placeholder="you@twico.dev" required />
<Input label="Search" leftIcon={<SearchIcon />} placeholder="Search…" />
<Input label="Amount" rightIcon={<span>USD</span>} placeholder="0.00" />
<Input label="Password" type="password" placeholder="••••••••" />
<Input label="Username" hint="Choose a unique handle" placeholder="twico" />
<Input label="Email" type="email" error="Invalid email address" defaultValue="nope" />
```

#### MultiSelect

MultiSelect is a MUI-Autocomplete-style multi-select where chosen values appear as removable chips and you type inline to filter a checkable options overlay. Use it when users need to pick several values from a list that benefits from search, grouped headings, or two-line (label + description) options.

`import { MultiSelect } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `options` — `Array<string | MultiSelectOption | MultiSelectGroup>` · **required** — Supplies the selectable options as plain strings, {value,label,description} objects, or {group,options} grouped sections.
- `label` — `React.ReactNode` — Renders a label node above the control so the multi-select has an accessible, visible name for the user.
- `hint` — `React.ReactNode` — Shows helper text below the control to guide selection; it is hidden once an error message is present.
- `error` — `React.ReactNode` — Displays a validation error below the control and styles it as invalid to flag a selection problem.
- `required` — `boolean` · default `false` — Marks the field as required and adds an asterisk to the label to signal a mandatory selection; defaults to false.
- `placeholder` — `string` · default `"Select…"` — Sets the prompt text shown when no chips are selected, defaulting to "Select…" to invite a choice.
- `value` — `string[]` — Controls the selected values as a string array, making the component fully controlled when paired with onChange.
- `defaultValue` — `string[]` · default `[]` — Seeds the initially selected values for uncontrolled usage, defaulting to an empty array of no selections.
- `onChange` — `(values: string[]) => void` — Fires with the new array of selected values whenever a chip is added or removed from the selection.
- `disabled` — `boolean` · default `false` — Disables the control and blocks all interaction when set to true, greying out the field; defaults to false.
- `size` — `"sm" | "md" | "lg"` · default `"md"` — Control height preset, matching Select/Combobox.
- `tone` — `"primary" | "success" | "warning" | "danger" | "info" | "neutral"` · default `"primary"` — Recolors the focus/open accent (border + ring) using one of six semantic intents; defaults to primary.
- `clearable` — `boolean` · default `false` — Show a clear-all (×) affix when at least one value is selected.
- `placement` — `"bottom" | "top"` · default `"bottom"` — Open the menu upward instead of down (e.g. near a viewport bottom).
- `portal` — `boolean` · default `false` — Render the dropdown in a portal (position:fixed on document.body) so it is never clipped by a scrolling/overflow-hidden ancestor; auto-flips up near the viewport bottom.
- `minWidth` — `number` · default `0` — Minimum popover width in px when portaled (useful when the control is narrow).
- `name` — `string` — Form field name submitted with the value.
- `readOnly` — `boolean` · default `false` — Makes the field non-editable while still focusable/selectable.
- `autoComplete` — `string` — Browser autocomplete hint (e.g. "email", "off").

```jsx
import { MultiSelect } from "twico-ui";

const [tags, setTags] = useState(["react", "tailwind"]);

<MultiSelect
  label="Tech stack"
  placeholder="Add technologies"
  value={tags}
  onChange={setTags}
  options={[
    { group: "Frameworks", options: [
      { value: "react", label: "React", description: "UI library" },
      { value: "vue", label: "Vue", description: "Progressive framework" },
    ]},
    { group: "Tooling", options: ["tailwind", "typescript"] },
  ]}
/>
```

#### Radio

Radio is a single radio button rendered with an optional label and description. Use several Radio inputs sharing the same `name` to let users pick exactly one option from a group.

`import { Radio } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `label` — `React.ReactNode` — Text or node rendered beside the radio dot, naming the option the user selects when choosing it.
- `description` — `React.ReactNode` — Secondary helper text shown under the label to add context or clarify what selecting the option means.
- `size` — `"sm" | "md"` · default `"md"` — Visual size of the radio control, sm or md, to align with the density of nearby form fields.
- `tone` — `"primary" | "success" | "warning" | "danger" | "info" | "neutral"` · default `"primary"` — Sets the accent color of the checked/active state from six semantic intents like success, danger, or info.
- `error` — `React.ReactNode` — Error message shown below the control; tints the dot red and wires aria-invalid/aria-describedby.
- `onChange` — `(e: React.ChangeEvent) => void` — Native change handler on the underlying form control.
- `name` — `string` — Form field name submitted with the value.
- `placeholder` — `string` — Placeholder text shown while the field is empty.
- `disabled` — `boolean` · default `false` — Disables the control — no interaction and a dimmed appearance.
- `readOnly` — `boolean` · default `false` — Makes the field non-editable while still focusable/selectable.
- `required` — `boolean` · default `false` — Marks the field required for native form validation.
- `autoComplete` — `string` — Browser autocomplete hint (e.g. "email", "off").

```jsx
import { Radio } from "twico-ui";

<Radio name="plan" value="free" label="Free" defaultChecked />
<Radio
  name="plan"
  value="pro"
  label="Pro"
  description="$12 / month"
/>
```

#### Rating

Rating is a star-rating input that works either as an interactive control (click or hover to set a score) or as a read-only display. Use it to capture user feedback like reviews or to show an existing score; clicking the current star again clears the rating to 0.

`import { Rating } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `value` — `number` — Controlled rating from 0 to count, letting the parent own the score and re-render when it changes.
- `defaultValue` — `number` · default `0` — Initial score for uncontrolled usage, defaulting to 0 so no stars start filled until the user interacts.
- `count` — `number` · default `5` — Total number of stars rendered, defaulting to 5, which sets the maximum selectable score.
- `size` — `"sm" | "md" | "lg"` · default `"md"` — Sets the star size (sm, md, or lg) to match the surrounding text density, defaulting to md.
- `tone` — `"primary" | "success" | "warning" | "danger" | "info" | "neutral"` · default `"warning"` — Sets the filled-star color from six semantic intents; defaults to warning (gold). The explicit color prop overrides it.
- `color` — `string` — Overrides the filled-star color with any CSS color, falling back to the warning/amber token when omitted.
- `readOnly` — `boolean` · default `false` — Renders the stars as a non-interactive display only, useful for showing an existing score without accepting input.
- `showValue` — `boolean` · default `false` — Displays the numeric score beside the stars when enabled, giving an exact figure alongside the visual rating.
- `onChange` — `(value: number) => void` — Fires with the new rating when the user clicks a star, or 0 when they clear the current selection.
- `disabled` — `boolean` · default `false` — Disable all interaction and dim the control.

```jsx
import { Rating } from "twico-ui";

// Interactive
const [r, setR] = React.useState(0);
<Rating value={r} onChange={setR} />

// Read-only display with numeric value
<Rating value={4} readOnly showValue />

// Uncontrolled, larger stars
<Rating defaultValue={3} size="lg" count={5} />
```

#### Select

A custom select that replaces the native browser dropdown with a rounded, keyboard-navigable popover, supporting grouped options and two-line (title + subtitle) options. Use it when you need a styled single-select with optional in-popover search, grouping, or descriptions.

`import { Select } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `label` — `React.ReactNode` — Renders a field label above the trigger to identify what the select controls within a form.
- `hint` — `React.ReactNode` — Shows helper text below the trigger to guide selection, automatically hidden once an error message is set.
- `error` — `React.ReactNode` — Displays an error message and marks the field invalid, replacing the hint to flag failed validation.
- `required` — `boolean` · default `false` — Appends a required asterisk to the label, signaling that a selection must be made before submitting.
- `size` — `"sm" | "md" | "lg"` · default `"md"` — Controls the trigger height (sm, md, or lg) to match the density of nearby form controls, defaulting to md.
- `tone` — `"primary" | "success" | "warning" | "danger" | "info" | "neutral"` · default `"primary"` — Recolors the focus/open accent (border + ring) using one of six semantic intents; defaults to primary.
- `placeholder` — `string` · default `"Select…"` — Text shown in the trigger when no value is selected, defaulting to "Select…" as a prompt to choose.
- `searchPlaceholder` — `string` · default `"Search…"` — Placeholder text for the in-popover search box, defaulting to "Search…" to invite filtering of options.
- `searchable` — `boolean` — Shows the filter search box inside the popover, auto-enabling when more than five options exist if left unset.
- `options` — `Array<string | SelectOption | SelectGroup>` · **required** — The selectable items as plain strings, {value,label,description} objects, or {group,options} groups for two-line or grouped lists.
- `value` — `string | null` — Controlled selected value as a string or null, letting the parent own which option is currently chosen.
- `defaultValue` — `string | null` · default `null` — Initial selected value for uncontrolled usage, defaulting to null so nothing is selected at first.
- `onChange` — `(value: string) => void` — Fires with the chosen value string when the user selects an option from the popover.
- `disabled` — `boolean` · default `false` — Disables the trigger so the popover cannot open and no selection can be made.
- `placement` — `"bottom" | "top"` · default `"bottom"` — Opens the menu downward or upward from the trigger, defaulting to "bottom" for the typical dropdown direction.
- `portal` — `boolean` · default `false` — Renders the dropdown in a body portal so it is never clipped, auto-flipping when near the viewport bottom.
- `minWidth` — `number` · default `0` — Sets the minimum popover width in pixels when portaled, ensuring the menu stays at least this wide.
- `clearable` — `boolean` · default `false` — Show a clear (×) affix when a value is selected; Delete/Backspace on the closed trigger also clears. Emits onChange(null).

```jsx
import { Select } from "twico-ui";

const [v, setV] = useState(null);

<Select
  label="Assignee"
  placeholder="Pick a teammate"
  value={v}
  onChange={setV}
  options={[
    { group: "Design", options: [
      { value: "ada", label: "Ada Park", description: "Product designer" },
      { value: "sam", label: "Sam Lee", description: "Brand designer" },
    ]},
    { group: "Engineering", options: [
      { value: "jo", label: "Jo Kim", description: "Frontend" },
    ]},
  ]}
/>
```

#### Slider

Slider is a range input with a filled track, draggable thumb, a value bubble that appears while dragging, optional step ticks, and full keyboard support (arrows, Home, End). Use it when users need to pick a number within a range, such as volume, price, or other continuous settings.

`import { Slider } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `label` — `React.ReactNode` — Renders a label above the track to describe what the slider adjusts, such as volume or price.
- `tone` — `"primary" | "success" | "warning" | "danger" | "info" | "neutral"` · default `"primary"` — Sets the filled-track and thumb accent color from six semantic intents; defaults to primary.
- `value` — `number` — Controlled current value, letting the parent own the slider position and re-render as the number changes.
- `defaultValue` — `number` · default `0` — Initial value for uncontrolled usage, defaulting to 0 so the thumb starts at the low end.
- `min` — `number` · default `0` — Lowest value the slider can reach, defaulting to 0, defining the start of the selectable range.
- `max` — `number` · default `100` — Highest value the slider can reach, defaulting to 100, defining the end of the selectable range.
- `step` — `number` · default `1` — Increment between selectable values, defaulting to 1, which controls how finely the thumb can be positioned.
- `disabled` — `boolean` · default `false` — Disables dragging and keyboard input while dimming the slider to show it is currently inactive.
- `showValue` — `boolean` · default `true` — Displays the current value at the top-right above the track when true (the default), for an always-visible readout.
- `showTicks` — `boolean` · default `false` — Renders step ticks along the rail when enabled, helping users see and snap to discrete increments.
- `formatValue` — `(value: number) => React.ReactNode` — Formats the displayed value in the label and drag bubble, useful for adding units, currency, or custom text.
- `onChange` — `(value: number) => void` — Fires with the new number whenever the value changes via drag or keyboard, keeping state in sync.
- `hint` — `React.ReactNode` — Helper text shown below the track when there is no error.
- `error` — `React.ReactNode` — Error message shown below the track; turns it red (sets aria-invalid) and replaces the hint.

```jsx
import { Slider } from "twico-ui";

const [vol, setVol] = React.useState(40);

<Slider label="Volume" value={vol} onChange={setVol} />

<Slider
  label="Price"
  min={0}
  max={1000}
  step={50}
  showTicks
  defaultValue={250}
  formatValue={(v) => `$${v}`}
/>
```

#### Switch

Switch is a toggle control with a springy thumb for instant on/off settings, optionally labeled with a title and supporting description. Use it for binary settings that apply immediately, like enabling dark mode or notifications.

`import { Switch } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `label` — `React.ReactNode` — Renders the title text shown beside the switch, naming the setting it toggles like "Enable notifications".
- `description` — `React.ReactNode` — Renders secondary helper text below the label to explain what the setting does or its consequences.
- `size` — `"sm" | "md"` · default `"md"` — Sets the visual size of the track and thumb (sm or md) to match the density of the surrounding form.
- `tone` — `"primary" | "success" | "warning" | "danger" | "info" | "neutral"` · default `"primary"` — Sets the ON-track accent color from six semantic intents; defaults to primary.
- `error` — `React.ReactNode` — Error message shown below the control; tints the track red and wires aria-invalid/aria-describedby.
- `onChange` — `(e: React.ChangeEvent) => void` — Native change handler on the underlying form control.
- `name` — `string` — Form field name submitted with the value.
- `placeholder` — `string` — Placeholder text shown while the field is empty.
- `disabled` — `boolean` · default `false` — Disables the control — no interaction and a dimmed appearance.
- `readOnly` — `boolean` · default `false` — Makes the field non-editable while still focusable/selectable.
- `required` — `boolean` · default `false` — Marks the field required for native form validation.
- `autoComplete` — `string` — Browser autocomplete hint (e.g. "email", "off").

```jsx
import { Switch } from "twico-ui";

<Switch label="Dark mode" defaultChecked />

<Switch
  label="Email notifications"
  description="Send me product updates"
/>

<Switch label="Compact view" size="sm" />
```

#### Textarea

Textarea is a multi-line text input with an optional label, hint text, and error state for longer-form content. Use it when collecting paragraph-length input like bios, descriptions, or comments.

`import { Textarea } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `label` — `React.ReactNode` — Renders a field label above the textarea, identifying what longer-form input the control collects.
- `hint` — `React.ReactNode` — Shows helper text below the textarea to guide input, hidden whenever an error message is present.
- `error` — `React.ReactNode` — Displays an error message and marks the field invalid when set, replacing the hint text below.
- `required` — `boolean` · default `false` — Adds a required asterisk beside the label when true, signaling that the field must be filled in.
- `size` — `"sm" | "md" | "lg"` · default `"md"` — Control size — same padding steps as Input.
- `tone` — `"primary" | "success" | "warning" | "danger" | "info" | "neutral"` · default `"primary"` — Recolors the focus/open accent (border + ring) using one of six semantic intents; defaults to primary.
- `onChange` — `(e: React.ChangeEvent) => void` — Native change handler on the underlying form control.
- `name` — `string` — Form field name submitted with the value.
- `placeholder` — `string` — Placeholder text shown while the field is empty.
- `rows` — `number` — Visible number of text rows.
- `disabled` — `boolean` · default `false` — Disables the control — no interaction and a dimmed appearance.
- `readOnly` — `boolean` · default `false` — Makes the field non-editable while still focusable/selectable.

```jsx
import { Textarea } from "twico-ui";

<Textarea
  label="Bio"
  rows={4}
  placeholder="Tell us about yourself"
  hint="Max 280 characters"
/>
```

### Data display

#### Avatar

Avatar displays a user's image with a graceful fallback to initials and an optional presence dot. Use it to represent people or entities in lists, headers, comments, and profile menus.

`import { Avatar } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `src` — `string` — The image URL to display, gracefully falling back to derived initials when it is missing or fails to load.
- `name` — `string` — The person's full name, used to derive the displayed initials and the avatar's accessible label.
- `size` — `"xs" | "sm" | "md" | "lg" | "xl"` · default `"md"` — Sets the overall avatar dimensions from xs to xl to suit lists, headers, comments, or profile menus.
- `square` — `boolean` · default `false` — When true, renders the avatar as a rounded square instead of the default circle for a different visual style.
- `status` — `"online" | "busy" | "away" | "offline"` — Shows a small presence dot indicating online, busy, away, or offline state on the avatar.
- `ring` — `boolean` · default `false` — When true, adds a brand-colored ring around the avatar to emphasize or highlight the user.

```jsx
import { Avatar } from "twico-ui";

<Avatar src="/u/jane.jpg" name="Jane Doe" />
<Avatar name="Sam Lee" status="online" />
<Avatar name="Twico" size="lg" square ring />
```

#### AvatarMenu

AvatarMenu is a user avatar that opens a portaled account dropdown with a rich header (avatar, name, email) above the menu items. Use it for the classic account menu — avatar-only in a navbar, or with name/email/chevron in a sidebar.

`import { AvatarMenu } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `name` — `string` — The user's full name, driving the derived initials and the rich header shown atop the dropdown.
- `src` — `string` — The avatar image URL shown in both the trigger and the dropdown header, falling back to initials when absent.
- `email` — `string` — The email address shown in the dropdown header, and in the trigger when showName is enabled.
- `subtitle` — `React.ReactNode` — Overrides the secondary line in the header and trigger, defaulting to the email when not provided.
- `status` — `"online" | "busy" | "away" | "offline"` — Shows a presence dot indicating online, busy, away, or offline state on the trigger avatar.
- `size` — `"xs" | "sm" | "md" | "lg" | "xl"` · default `"sm"` — Sets the trigger avatar dimensions from xs to xl to fit a compact navbar or a roomier sidebar.
- `items` — `MenuItemDef[]` · **required** — The account menu entries to render in the dropdown, using the same MenuItemDef shape as Menu's items.
- `showName` — `boolean` · default `false` — When true, displays the name and email beside the avatar in the trigger instead of showing the avatar alone.
- `showChevron` — `boolean` · default `showName` — When true, shows a chevron in the trigger to hint at the dropdown, defaulting to the showName value.
- `align` — `"start" | "end"` · default `"end"` — Aligns the dropdown's start or end edge to the trigger, defaulting to end for right-anchored account menus.

```jsx
import { AvatarMenu } from "twico-ui";

<AvatarMenu
  name="Ada Park"
  email="ada@twico.dev"
  status="online"
  showName
  items={[
    { label: "Profile", onClick: () => {} },
    { label: "Settings", shortcut: "⌘,", onClick: () => {} },
    { separator: true },
    { label: "Sign out", danger: true, onClick: () => {} },
  ]}
/>
```

#### Badge

Badge is a compact status label or count chip available in six semantic tones and three fill styles. Use it to flag state (active, pending, draft), categories, or small counts inline next to text, list items, or table rows.

`import { Badge } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `tone` — `"primary" | "success" | "warning" | "danger" | "info" | "neutral"` · default `"primary"` — Sets the semantic color from six options like success or danger to convey the badge's state or category.
- `variant` — `"soft" | "solid" | "outline"` · default `"soft"` — Chooses the fill style between soft, solid, and outline to control how prominently the badge reads.
- `size` — `"sm" | "md" | "lg"` · default `"md"` — Adds a new "sm" option (smaller font padding/height than md) to the existing size scale; md/lg unchanged and md remains the default.
- `dot` — `boolean` · default `false` — When true, shows a small leading status dot before the label to reinforce the badge's state.
- `children` — `React.ReactNode` — The badge content, typically a short status label or a small count shown inline next to text.

```jsx
import { Badge } from "twico-ui";

<Badge tone="success" dot>Active</Badge>
<Badge tone="warning" variant="solid">Pending</Badge>
<Badge tone="neutral" variant="outline">Draft</Badge>
<Badge tone="info" size="lg">12 new</Badge>
```

#### Card

Card is a surface container for grouping related content, with an optional header (title/subtitle), body, and footer. Use it to present grouped information such as stats, summaries, or actions, optionally making it interactive with a hover-lift effect.

`import { Card } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `title` — `React.ReactNode` — Renders a bold title in the card header, giving the grouped content a clear heading at the top.
- `subtitle` — `React.ReactNode` — Renders a muted subtitle beneath the title to add supporting context or a brief secondary description.
- `footer` — `React.ReactNode` — Renders pinned footer content such as action buttons below the card body, separated from the main content.
- `variant` — `"elevated" | "outline" | "soft"` · default `"elevated"` — Selects the surface treatment from elevated, outline, or soft to control how the card stands out from the page.
- `padding` — `"none" | "md" | "lg"` · default `"md"` — Sets the card's inner padding to none, md, or lg, with none useful for edge-to-edge media or custom layouts.
- `interactive` — `boolean` · default `false` — When true, adds a hover-lift effect and pointer cursor to signal the whole card is clickable.
- `fullHeight` — `boolean` · default `false` — Stretches the card to fill its parent cell (height: 100%) so cards in a grid or flex row align to equal height.
- `children` — `React.ReactNode` — The main body content of the card, rendered between the optional header and footer regions.

```jsx
import { Card } from "twico-ui";

<Card
  title="Monthly revenue"
  subtitle="June 2026"
  footer={<Button size="sm">View report</Button>}
>
  Revenue grew 18% month over month.
</Card>

<Card variant="outline" interactive>
  Hover me — I lift.
</Card>
```

#### Carousel

Carousel is a sliding one-slide-per-view component with prev/next arrows, dot indicators, optional looping, and optional autoplay that pauses on hover. Use it to showcase a sequence of images or content panels where each child is a single slide.

`import { Carousel } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `showArrows` — `boolean` · default `true` — When true, displays prev/next arrow controls so users can step manually between slides in either direction.
- `showDots` — `boolean` · default `true` — When true, displays dot indicators beneath the slides showing position and letting users jump to a specific slide.
- `loop` — `boolean` · default `true` — When true, wraps navigation around the ends so advancing past the last slide returns to the first and vice versa.
- `autoPlay` — `boolean` · default `false` — When true, automatically advances slides on a timer, pausing while the pointer hovers over the carousel.
- `interval` — `number` · default `4000` — Sets the delay in milliseconds between automatic slide advances when autoPlay is enabled, defaulting to 4000.
- `children` — `React.ReactNode` — The slide elements, where each direct child is rendered as a single full-width slide in the carousel.
- `index` — `number` — Controlled active slide index — pair with onIndexChange.
- `defaultIndex` — `number` · default `0` — Initial slide index when uncontrolled.
- `onIndexChange` — `(index: number) => void` — Fired when the active slide changes (arrows, dots, or autoplay).

```jsx
import { Carousel } from "twico-ui";

<Carousel autoPlay interval={5000}>
  <img src="/a.jpg" alt="" />
  <img src="/b.jpg" alt="" />
  <div>Any slide content</div>
</Carousel>
```

#### Chart

A lightweight, dependency-free SVG chart that renders bar or line graphs (single or multi-series) with grid, axes, animated entrance, tooltips, and an optional legend. Use it to visualize small datasets like daily metrics or trends without pulling in a charting library.

`import { Chart } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `type` — `"bar" | "line"` · default `"bar"` — Selects the rendering style, drawing the data as grouped bars or as connected lines for trends over time.
- `data` — `ChartDatum[]` · **required** — Supplies the array of data points, each carrying a label plus one numeric field for every named series.
- `series` — `string[]` · default `["value"]` — Names the numeric fields to plot, where multiple keys yield grouped bars or multiple lines per data point.
- `height` — `number` · default `220` — Sets the chart's pixel height, controlling its vertical footprint while it scales horizontally to its container.
- `showGrid` — `boolean` · default `true` — Draws horizontal grid lines behind the data, on by default, to make values easier to read against the scale.
- `showAxis` — `boolean` · default `true` — Renders axis tick marks and label text, enabled by default, so viewers can map plotted values to categories.
- `showLegend` — `boolean` · default `false` — Displays a legend keying each series to its color, off by default and most useful when plotting multiple series.
- `valueFormat` — `(value: number) => string` — Formats raw numeric values into display strings for tooltips, letting you add units, separators, or currency symbols.
- `colors` — `string[]` · default `built-in token palette` — Per-series colors (any CSS color), cycled when shorter than series.
- `ariaLabel` — `string` — Accessible name for the chart's <svg role="img">; defaults to a description of the chart type.

```jsx
import { Chart } from "twico-ui";

<Chart
  type="bar"
  data={[
    { label: "Mon", value: 240 },
    { label: "Tue", value: 310 },
    { label: "Wed", value: 280 },
  ]}
  valueFormat={(v) => `$${v}`}
/>

<Chart
  type="line"
  series={["signups", "active"]}
  showLegend
  data={[
    { label: "Jan", signups: 120, active: 80 },
    { label: "Feb", signups: 180, active: 140 },
  ]}
/>
```

#### Datatable
